free_text: Measurement and evaluation of the cross-section for
helium elastic scattering from nitrogen
6.0 MV Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator
A thin self-supporting SiN membrane window with
nominal thickness of 100 nm.
On the top, a thin Au layer was evaporated for
normalization purposes
Pure Rutherford scattering assumed at measured angles
Three surface barrier detectors with a 2.5
msr solid angle each were positioned at 118., 150.
and 165.degr. Alphas, delimited by horizontal and
vertical slits to a spot of 2x2 mm2 impinged on the
sample at normal incidence. The energy resolution of
the detectors used was between 15-20 keV for alphas
in the measured energy range.
The following sources of uncertainties were cancelled
due to ratio measurement:
- Uncertainties due to dead time, solid angle;
- Improper charge measurement.
The following source of the uncertainty was ignored:
- Detector angular settings
Entry approved by Dr.I.Bogdanovic Radovic
SD
On. AUTHOR and REFERENCE updated
The following factors were considered:
Counting statistics of the peak;
Uncertainty in sample atom number ratio (Au/N)
Data from I.Bogdanovic Radovic
